volumeTitle: Birds. Lysistrata. Women at the Thesmophoria
label: Birds (Section 1.1685 - Section 1765)

sections.label: Section 1.1685
sections.original: ἡμῖν ἃ λέγεις σὺ πάντα συγχωρεῖν δοκεῖ .
sections.translation: We are of a mind to grant you all that you ask . But come up there with us to receive Basileia and the celestial bounty .

sections.label: Section 1.1688
sections.translation: Here are birds already dressed , and very suitable for a nuptial feast .

sections.label: Section 1.1689b
sections.translation: You go and , if you like , I will stay here to roast them .

sections.label: Section 1.1691
sections.original: ὀπτᾷς τὰ κρέα ; πολλήν γε τενθείαν λέγεις .
sections.translation: You to roast them ? you are too much the glutton ; come along with us .

sections.label: Section 1.1692b
sections.translation: Ah ! how well I would have treated myself !

sections.label: Section 1.1693
sections.original: ἀλλὰ γαμικὴν χλανίδα δότω τις δεῦρό μοι .
sections.translation: Let some one bring me a beautiful and magnificent tunic for the wedding .

sections.label: Section 1.1694
sections.original: ἔστι δʼ ἐν Φαναῖσι πρὸς τῇ
sections.translation: At Phanae , near the

sections.label: Section 1.1695
sections.translation: Clepsydra , there dwells a people who have neither faith nor law , the Englottogastors , who reap , sow , pluck the vines and the figs with their tongues ;

sections.label: Section 1.1700
sections.translation: they belong to a barbaric race , and among them the Philippi and the Gorgiases are to be found ; ' tis these Englottogastorian Philippi who introduced the custom all over Attica

sections.label: Section 1.1705
sections.translation: of cutting out the tongue separately at sacrifices .

sections.label: Section 1706
sections.original: ὦ πάντʼ ἀγαθὰ πράττοντες , ὦ μείζω λόγου ,
sections.translation: Oh , you , whose unbounded happiness I cannot express in words , thrice happy race of airy birds , receive your king in your fortunate dwellings . More brilliant than the brightest

sections.label: Section 1710
sections.translation: star that illumes the earth , he is approaching his glittering golden palace ; the sun itself does not shine with more dazzling glory . He is entering with his bride at his side , whose beauty no human tongue can express ; in his hand he brandishes the lightning , the winged shaft of Zeus ;

sections.label: Section 1715
sections.original: ὀσμὴ δʼ ἀνωνόμαστος ἐς βάθος κύκλου
sections.translation: perfumes of unspeakable sweetness pervade the ethereal realms . ' Tis a glorious spectacle to see the clouds of incense wafting in light whirlwinds before the breath of the zephyr ! But here he is himself . Divine Muse ! let thy sacred lips begin with songs of happy omen .

sections.label: Section 1720
sections.translation: Fall back ! to the right ! to the left ! advance ! Fly around this happy mortal , whom Fortune loads with her blessings . Oh ! oh ! what grace ! what beauty !

sections.label: Section 1725
sections.original: ὦ μακαριστὸν σὺ γάμον τῇδε πόλει γημας .
sections.translation: Oh , marriage so auspicious for our city !

sections.label: Section 1726
sections.translation: All honor to this man ! ' tis through him that the birds are called to such glorious destinies . Let your nuptial hymns , your nuptial songs , greet

sections.label: Section 1730
sections.translation: him and his Basileia !

sections.label: Section 1.1731
sections.translation: [*] ' Twas that the Fates formerly united Olympian Hera to the King who governs the gods from the summit of his inaccessible throne

sections.label: Section 1.1735
sections.translation: in the midst of such festivities . Oh ! Hymen ! oh ! Hymenaeus !

sections.label: Section 1.1737
sections.translation: Rosy Eros with the golden wings held the reins and guided the chariot ; ' twas he , who presided over

sections.label: Section 1.1740
sections.translation: the union of Zeus and the fortunate Hera . Oh ! Hymen ! oh ! Hymenaeus !

sections.label: Section 1743
sections.translation: I am delighted with your songs , I applaud your verses . Now celebrate

sections.label: Section 1745
sections.translation: the thunder that shakes the earth , the flaming lightning of Zeus and the terrible flashing thunderbolt .

sections.label: Section 1748
sections.translation: Oh , thou golden flash of the lightning ! oh , ye divine shafts of flame , that Zeus has hitherto shot forth !

sections.label: Section 1750
sections.translation: Oh , ye rolling thunders , that bring down the rain ! ' Tis by the order of our king that ye shall now stagger the earth ! Oh , Hymen ! ' tis through thee that he commands the universe and that he makes Basileia , whom he has robbed from Zeus , take her seat at his side . Oh ! Hymen ! oh ! Hymenaeus !

sections.label: Section 1755
sections.translation: Let all the winged tribes of our fellow-citizens follow the bridal couple to the palace of Zeus and to the nuptial couch ! Stretch forth your

sections.label: Section 1760
sections.translation: hands , my dear wife ! Take hold of me by my wings and let us dance ; I am going to lift you up and carry you through the air .

sections.label: Section 1763
sections.translation: Alalalai ie Paian ! Tenella kallinikos !

sections.label: Section 1765
sections.translation: Loftiest art thou of gods !
